background.experience: tracking specimens, controls, invalid runs, and corrective action across regulated diagnostic workflows
background.attention_bias: chain of custody, missing-result detection, and invalid-run handling
background.counter_bias: assuming automation completeness from a green summary

readers.thesis: Test sovereignty exists when Foundation can prove that every required examination ran against the admitted artifact under the declared environment and that missing, retried, quarantined, or infrastructure-failed results remain visible.
observations: Earlier runs separate CI execution from release authority and artifact custody.
The repository currently contains overlapping workflows and broad gates that can duplicate work while obscuring exact coverage.
uncertainties: No measured test dependency graph or flake baseline exists for the full repository.

background.experience: dependency graphs, hermetic test actions, remote execution, and proof-carrying build evidence
background.attention_bias: graph completeness, hidden inputs, and cache-key correctness
background.counter_bias: equating deterministic execution with semantic test adequacy

readers.thesis: A sovereign test orchestrator compiles policy into a content-addressed test plan, executes each node hermetically, and rejects release when graph coverage, attempt semantics, or evidence identity diverges from the admitted plan.
observations: Bazel defines isolated test environments, sharding contracts, timeouts, and undeclared-output handling.
Remote Execution APIs separate action execution and content-addressed results from any one worker implementation.
uncertainties: Existing JavaScript, Playwright, Node, and Python tests are not yet normalized into one target graph.
Coverage adequacy remains a policy question beyond execution completeness.

agreements: Make the test-plan digest part of release evidence.
Reject missing targets, missing shards, infrastructure failures, and artifact-digest mismatches.
Record every attempt and prohibit silent retry-based success.
disagreements: Reader A prioritizes visible invalidation and operator-readable evidence; Reader B prioritizes graph compilation, hermetic actions, and content-addressed replay.
omissions_detected: Previous architecture did not define a canonical test-plan manifest distinct from CI workflow files.
No existing gate distinguishes product failure, test flake, infrastructure failure, quarantine, and not-run states.
revisions_after_discussion: Add an explicit result-state algebra and completeness validator.
Keep Bazel and remote execution as replaceable implementations while Foundation owns test policy, plan compilation, and release acceptance.

notes_added.statement: Treat a test run as an admitted evidence graph: every required test target, environment, attempt, shard, artifact digest, and result must be accounted for before a release gate can pass.
Treat retries and quarantines as diagnostic evidence, never as silent conversion of an unreliable test into a passing release signal.

notes_challenged.challenge: Disposable workers remain insufficient unless Foundation verifies that the complete admitted test plan ran and that retries, skipped targets, and infrastructure failures remain explicit.

privacy.review: Contains only abstract architecture, synthetic reader profiles, coverage boundaries, and non-identifying continuity findings. Excludes private source material and personal data.
